text: Science has a clearer story about Andromeda's approach than social media does. The Andromeda Galaxy is on a collision course with our Milky Way, but what does this really mean? In about 4.5 billion years, they’ll merge, forming a new galaxy. But it won’t be a fiery disaster. Instead, stars are so far apart that they likely won’t collide. It’s more like a cosmic dance. The real drama? New star formations from gas clouds, creating stunning light shows over millions of years. Curious about more cosmic events? Hit that follow for more science mysteries!
